2603.17146.title: Multilingual Reference Need Assessment System for Wikipedia
2603.17146.paper_summary: Wikipedia is a critical source of information for millions of users across the Web. It serves as a key resource for large language models, search engines, question-answering systems, and other Web-based applications. In Wikipedia, content needs to be verifiable, meaning that readers can check that claims are backed by references to reliable sources. This depends on manual verification by editors, an effective but labor-intensive process, especially given the high volume of daily edits. To address this challenge, we introduce a multilingual machine learning system to assist editors in identifying claims requiring citations. Our approach is tested in 10 language editions of Wikipedia, outperforming existing benchmarks for reference need assessment. We not only consider machine learning evaluation metrics but also system requirements, allowing us to explore the trade-offs between model accuracy and computational efficiency under real-world infrastructure constraints. We deploy our system in production and release data and code to support further research.
2603.17146.paper_summary_zh: 維基百科是數百萬網路使用者的重要信息來源。它作為大型語言模型、搜索引擎、問答系統和其他基於網路的應用程序的關鍵資源。在維基百科中，內容需要可驗證，這意味著讀者可以檢查主張是否有可靠來源的參考支持。這依賴於編輯的手動驗證，這是一個有效但勞動密集的過程，特別是考慮到每日編輯的高量。為了解決這一挑戰，我們引入了一個多語言機器學習系統，以協助編輯識別需要引用的主張。我們的方法在維基百科的10種語言版本中進行測試，超越了現有的參考需求評估基準。我們不僅考慮機器學習評估指標，還考慮系統需求，使我們能夠在現實基礎設施限制下探索模型準確性和計算效率之間的權衡。我們將系統部署到生產環境中，並釋放數據和代碼以支持進一步的研究。
2603.17146.author: Aitolkyn Baigutanova et.al.
2603.17146.authors: Aitolkyn Baigutanova, Francisco Navas, Pablo Aragon, Mykola Trokhymovych, Muniza Aslam, Ai-Jou Chou, Miriam Redi, Diego Saez-Trumper
